summary:
Combining antiretrovirals in a single dose improves adherence by simplifying treatment regimen, leading to better viral suppression and fewer side effects. This enhances overall quality of life through improved disease management and patient compliance.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$

Okay, let me go back over this again with full attention—this time focusing only on what is clinically essential:

- Fixed-dose combinations improve medication adherence due to simpler dosing schedules.
- Better adherence results in superior viral load reduction, lower rates of drug resistance, and decreased morbidity—all directly linked to higher quality of life in people living with HIV.

Therefore, yes — such formulations are associated with meaningful improvements in real-world care outcomes that support choice A.
